comments.body: Try renaming the method to fun findByOriginalA_Id(originalAId: Long): BModel? if it works I will add an answer to you

answers.body:
I think I understand why it's not working.

When using sprint 3.3.6 with hibernate 6.5 the mehod in question executes this SQL

select spm1_0.id,spm1_0.created_at,spm1_0.network_transaction_reference,spm1_0.original_donation_id,spm1_0.original_donation_ip,spm1_0.person_id,spm1_0.retry_attempts,spm1_0.scheduled_payment_type,spm1_0.status,spm1_0.status_detail,spm1_0.updated_at,spm1_0.uuid from scheduled_payments spm1_0 where spm1_0.original_donation_id=?

Now, with the upgraded dependencies and the change from Long to Long? it makes this query

select spm1_0.id,spm1_0.created_at,spm1_0.network_transaction_reference,spm1_0.original_donation_id,spm1_0.original_donation_ip,spm1_0.person_id,spm1_0.retry_attempts,spm1_0.scheduled_payment_type,spm1_0.status,spm1_0.status_detail,spm1_0.updated_at,spm1_0.uuid from scheduled_payments spm1_0 left join donations od1_0 on od1_0.id=spm1_0.original_donation_id where od1_0.id=?

It's now making a join for some reason.

With the query annotation I mentioned it executes the same SQL as it did before.

I'm not sure if the dependency upgrade of the change from Long to Long? caused this, will update if at one point I find that out.

As I was upgrading the version, I found that a left join was added

when changing from org.hibernate.orm:hibernate-core:6.4.10.Final to org.hibernate.orm:hibernate-core:6.5.0.CR1

title: Spring repository: find by id on related table
body:
I have two models (written in Kotlin):

@Entity
@Table(name = "a")
class AModel(

    @Id
    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    var id: Long? = null,
)

and a second one with a one to one relation to this previos one

@Entity
@Table(name = "b")
class BModel(
    @Id
    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    var id: Long? = null,

    @field:OneToOne
    @JoinColumn(name = "original_a_id")
    var originalA: AModel? = null,
)

Then in BRepository I have a method to fetch a instance of B by the id of the related model A.

fun findByOriginalAId(originalAId: Long): BModel?

That until recently it worked correctly, but I'm trying to upgrade my app to Spring 3.4 and latest version of Hibernate and suddenly this is a problem. This method on the repository no longer finds the model. The only change I implemented was to change the type of the id fields to Long? and default to null, and deleage to hibernate the actual value generation, which I read I need to do.

I found a workaround for the repository method to annotate it with

@Query("SELECT b FROM BModel b WHERE b.originalA.id = :originalAId")

But why do I suddenly need to do this? Is there a way to obtain the same result without the cumbersome annotation?
